route: Manhattan Bridge to Prospect Park and back
reflections: My leg didnt hurt for the first time in months. Stretching must be working.
First hot day of the year. Didnt feel like I was running very well, but my legs didnt hurt.
First sweaty run of the year, bicycle bros were back, gut felt like a bowling ball, no leg pain

route: Up the East River to Randalls Island and back
reflections: I noticed that the footbridge at 72nd Street is still closed

route: Manhattan Bridge and back on the Brooklyn Bridge
reflections: Legs felt great, sped up at the end, nice and cool in the 30s.
Beautiful spring morning, legs felt good.

route: Queensborough Bridge and back on the Williamsburg Bridge
reflections: Legs felt great, cool weather, nearly fell into the Brooklyn Experience half

route: Down and around the Battery, then back on 12th Street
reflections: Dark skies, but it didn’t rain.
Tired after a concert last night but drug my ass over and back on a beautiful morning.

route: Manhattan Bridge to Prospect Park and back
reflections: Legs felt good. A little stomach nausea. Need to remember to hydrate.

route: Up around Central Park, around the reservoir, and down West Side Highway to 17th Street and home
Over the Manhattan Bridge, a lap around Prospect Park and then a straight shot to the Coney Island Boardwalk
reflections: My first run from home to Coney Island

route: 59th Street Bridge to Hunter's Point and back
Up Central Park to 86th Street, down the West Side Highway, and home on 29th Street
59th Street Bridge to Hunter's Point and back
Up Broadway and the Harlem River Greenway, across the High Bridge, down the Joker stairs to Yankee Stadium
East River up to 86th Street, over to 3rd Avenue and back home
reflections: First time I ran up the new foot bridge up to 71th Street after renovations at HSS

route: Manhattan Bridge → Prospect Park → back across Manhattan Bridge
Manhattan Bridge through Fort Greene, back on the Williamsburg Bridge
